$comment: REFERENCE SCENARIO — every future scenario is cargo-culted from this one. If you change the schema, update SCENARIO_ENGINE_SPEC.md first, then this file, then run the headless solver smoke test in CI. All player-facing copy in `voice.*` must satisfy CONCEPT.md Voice constraints.

meta.title: MAKE THE SHOT.
meta.subtitle: FREE THROW — LEVEL 01
meta.authorIntent: First exposure to projectile motion. Must feel friendly. The textbook-classical answer at θ=52° (solving y(t) = h_h via t = d/(v·cos θ) substitution) is v ≈ 7.52 m/s — this is what the student derives if they apply the equations in `hints[2].body`. The simulation accepts this answer as SWISH because the rim geometry is calibrated to match the textbook math: rim contact only registers when the ball center is at the rim plane (within rimThickness), not for fly-over proximity. **The pedagogical rule is sacred: scenario answers come from real physics formulae.** If you change ball/hoop/release geometry, re-derive the textbook answer and update smokeTest.answer + ghostArc.answer.

situation.questionRevealed: Find an angle θ and velocity v that lands the ball through the hoop.

subheadVariants.1: Came up short. Not enough lift.
subheadVariants.2: Still short. Push the arc higher.

subheadVariants.1: Front rim. Angle too low. Arc was too flat.
subheadVariants.2: Still front rim. Push the arc higher.
subheadVariants.3+: Front rim again. Lift the trajectory.

subheadVariants.2: Still back rim. Cut the velocity.

subheadVariants.1: Overshot the rim. Too much velocity.
subheadVariants.3+: Far overshoot. Velocity well above threshold.

subheadVariants.2: Air ball again. Aim for the rim.
subheadVariants.3+: Still air ball. Check the angle and velocity.

ghostArc.description: The textbook-classical answer at θ=52°. Derived from y(t) = h_h equation: with h_r=2.0, h_h=3.05, d=4.6, g=9.8, substituting t = d/(v·cos θ) gives v ≈ 7.52 m/s. The simulation accepts this as a clean SWISH. Visually obvious as 'the right shape' even to a player who doesn't know the math.

hints.body: Topic: projectile motion. The ball is in free fall the moment it leaves your hand.

Two equations, two unknowns:
  x(t) = v·cos(θ)·t
  y(t) = h_r + v·sin(θ)·t − ½·g·t²
The ball reaches the hoop when x(t) = d and y(t) = h_h.

Eliminate t: substitute t = d / (v·cos θ) into the y equation.
You'll get one equation in θ and v — pick any θ between 45°–60° and solve for v.

workedSteps: Solve y(t) = h_h for v → v ≈ 7.52 m/s.

intro.headline: MAKE THE SHOT.
intro.subhead: Find the angle. Find the velocity.

subheadByFlavor.RIM_DROP: Lucky bounce. The math was close.

miss.headline: MISSED

bracketHintByCategory.SHORT: Geometry: θ near 52°, v above 7.5 m/s.
bracketHintByCategory.AIRBALL: Geometry: θ near 52°, v near 7.5 m/s.

miss.afterAllHintsCopy: All hints revealed. The math is on the table.

smokeTest.$comment: Required by CI. The engine runs the simulation with this answer and asserts the success predicate fires. Schema-valid scenarios that fail the smoke test fail the build.
